sourceMovie.title: Rocky III
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Rocky III (1982) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Creed II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rocky III clicked for you, Creed II (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Rocky III?
faq.answer: Try Creed II, Creed III, Letters from Iwo Jima—each shares DNA with Rocky III while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Rocky III?
faq.answer: Rocky III (1982) is best described as Drama, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Rocky III part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Rocky Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
